# Promissory Note — Cousin Scenario

**IMPORTANT:** This template is for educational reference only. Consult an attorney before using any promissory note. FHA has specific rules about undisclosed secondary financing.

---

**PROMISSORY NOTE**

**Date:** [Date]
**Principal Amount:** $[Amount]
**Location:** [City, State]

**Borrower:** [Full Legal Name], residing at [Address]

**Lender:** [Full Legal Name], residing at [Address]

---

FOR VALUE RECEIVED, Borrower promises to pay to Lender the principal sum of **$[Amount]**, together with interest at the rate of **[X]% per annum** on the unpaid balance, payable as follows:

[Choose one:]

**Option A — Installment:**
Monthly installments of $[Amount] beginning [Date], and continuing on the same day of each month thereafter until paid in full.

**Option B — Balloon:**
The full principal and accrued interest shall be due and payable on [Date].

**Option C — Interest Only with Balloon:**
Monthly interest-only payments of $[Amount] beginning [Date], with the full principal balance due on [Date].

---

**Prepayment:** Borrower may prepay this note in whole or in part at any time without penalty.

**Default:** If Borrower fails to make any payment within [15] days of the due date, the entire unpaid balance and accrued interest shall become immediately due and payable at Lender's option.

**Governing Law:** This note shall be governed by the laws of the State of [State].

---

**Borrower Signature:** _____________________________ Date: __________

**Borrower Printed Name:** _____________________________

---

## FHA Disclosure Warning

FHA requires **full disclosure of all secondary financing** at application. If this note is secured by the property, it must be disclosed to the primary lender. Undisclosed secondary liens are mortgage fraud.

- If the note is **unsecured** and payments are deferred until after closing with no lien on the property, it may be treated as a gift by the lender — but confirm with your loan officer.
- Reference: HUD Handbook 4000.1, Section II.A.4.c (Secondary Financing)

## Notes
- Have both parties sign in front of a notary if the amount is significant.
- Record the note in county records if secured by real property.
- Consult a real estate attorney in your state.
